where.text: Where have you been? You're unbelievably late.

ready.text: I was just getting ready. Marcelle was helping me chose an outfit.

ready2.text: Well it looks atrocious. Your hair is still out of control. Do I have to hire a new butler? What is he even good at.

ready3.text: I think he did a good job with the outfit. I mean, I like it.

ready4.text: Quiet you! You are already on thin ice. You must be perfect tonight. Your Father and I will be watching you.

silence2.text: On top of this, you look terrible. What a mess. Did you even try taming your hair?

fix.text: There. That is much better. Now you look like the civilized man should be. Isn't that right. Honey?

fix2.text: I agree, my darling. Much more refined. Perfect for his job tonight.

fix4.text: I have decided that you are hosting at tonight's gala. After yesterday's... fiasco, you must be on the your best behavior. Your Father and I will be watching you.

defend.text: I think it looks nice. It looks like me.

hair.text: Your Mother commented on your hair again?

truth.text: Do you think my parents hate me?

truth2.text: No. They-they don't. They're just hard on you because they want you to succeed.

truth3.text: I just want to make them proud. They said I had to host tonight.

truth4.text: Well, I have a few ideas. I used to host back in my day too, you know.

truth6.text: Yeah. At your Grandparent's events, I always had to serve the guests. They always said I needed to practice my manners. Unlike you, I was a rebel.

truth8.text: Woah, woah. I'm lucky your parents can't hear you right now. They already think I'm a bad influence.

truth9.text: I think you're the best, Uncle.

lie.text: Okay, Kiki. If you say so.

lie2.text: My parents just asked me to be a host. I'm not sure how to be a host, or what that really is.

lie3.text: It's pretty simple, really. I used to do it when I was your age at our family dinners.

lie5.text: I'm just yanking on your chain. You're cool to me.

lie6.text: You're cool to me too. The coolest.

attentive.text: Anyway, we're getting off topic. To be the perfect host, you must be attentive.

attentive3.text: Attentive. It means you have to make sure they guests always have what they need. Bring them food and drinks. How about you go grab that tray on the serving table.

notray.text: The tray is right over there by on the serving table. Grab that and bring it back to me. Okay, kiddo?

tray.text: Great! Now that you have the tray, go to the other table and grab a few drinks. Not too many that you can't carry the tray, okay?

nodrinks.text: Get the drinks from the other table over there. You got this, Kiki!

drinks.text: Perfect! Just hand out these drinks to guests. Here, I'll be your first guest. Give me a drink.

drinks2.text: Here, sir. It looks like you are in need of a drink.

drinks3.text: Why thank you, young man. This party is better all ready. Now, just do the same for the food.

drinks5.text: My final tip, talk to the guests. Make them feel welcome. If you follow these, you'll surely succeed.

drinks6.text: Thank you, Uncle. You're the best.

drinks7.text: Oh! One more thing. Marcelle gave this to me earlier. I'm not sure why he did in retrospect. I think it is from a friend of yours. Here. Now, go knock them dead, Kiki!

beverage.text: Hello, sir. Would you like a beverage?

beverage2.text: Uh, sure. Not everyday the only son of the Percival family hands me a drink at his own "charity" gala.

beverage3.text: I'm sorry, sir. What do you mean by that?

beverage4.text: This event your family put on. It's all for show.

nobeverage.text: Hello, sir. Would you like a beverage?

nobeverage2.text: What is this? Are your parents playing some kind of joke on me? You do not have refreshments.

nobeverage3.text: My deepest apologies, sir. I will go grab some right away.

charity.text: No. This is a charity event for people who cannot afford our services. So that they can get it.

charity2.text: Is that what your parents told you?

charity3.text: Yes and it is the truth.

charity4.text: I'm sorry, child. This gala is not what your parents say it is. All of the money earned tonight will be lining their pockets. They want to buy another Zeppelin.

charity6.text: I am their financier. I keep track of their money.

charity8.text: Do not tell them I said any of this. I spoke of nothing you understood.

question2.text: This is all a front. Your parents are pocketing the money earned. They want it all to themselves.

question4.text: Believe what you want for I speak the truth to you. I know your parents well. Now go bother someone else, child.

food.text: Hello, miss. Would you like some Coquilles Saint-Jacques?

food2.text: That sounds delicious. I will have one. Thank you, sir.

nofood.text: Hello, miss. Would you like some Coquilles Saint-Jacques?

nofood2.text: Sir, you do not have anything on the tray.

wallet.text: A wallet? Someone must have lost it. I must keep it somewhere safe until I find them.

outfit.text: I heard that your mother did not like your outfit. I must do better next time.

outfit3.text: Thank you, young master. I appreciate your kind words.

candles.text: The candles are low. I must go replace them, young master.

alcohol.text: Did that drink have alcohol in it? Rinka said I could have the tall glasses.

already.text: Your Mother already told you what you should be doing.

cater.text: What are you doing? Go cater to our guests.

goingon.text: What is going on over there?
